mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.3pp, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and business demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns (positive returns in >50% of cases) or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the index of industrial production above 0.5% signals robust manufacturing output and supply-side strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth >0.5% is followed by negative 5-day returns in >55% of cases during early-cycle expansions, or rolling IC drops below 0.015.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly acceleration in the consumer price index above 0.4% signals rising inflation pressures that force markets to price in tighter monetary policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL MoM > 0.4% is followed by positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate indicates labor force participation shocks and weak job quality, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS/UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative returns (positive in >50% of cases) or the interaction effect is insignificant in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product above 2.0% reflects expanding economic output and corporate earnings potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth >2.0% is followed by negative 20-day returns in >55% of quarters, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.015 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production below -0.2% during periods of high volatility signals supply chain disruptions and reduced capacity utilization, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO contraction < -0.2% yields positive 5-day returns in >55% of high-volatility periods, or signal IC < 0.01 in out-of-sample tests.
